---
title: "Prompt engineering and RAG"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 50%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Hello!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

## Day 1: Build on AI

1.  Talking with LLMs via code 
2.  Programming with LLMs 
3.  Prompt engineering and RAG (this session!)
4.  Beyond prompts

# Prompt engineering and hallucinations {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

## Models will confidently make things up

-   What hallucination looks like
-   Why a confident answer is not a correct answer

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `09-plot-interpret` and ask the model about a plot:

-   Generate a plot of mpg vs. weight from `mtcars`
-   Ask the model to interpret it
-   Discuss what it got right and where it leaned on priors
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `10-plot-noise`:

-   Replace the plot with random noise; keep the prompt the same
-   See what the model says
-   Pair up with a neighbor and try to steer the model into an "appropriate" interpretation of pure noise
:::

{{< countdown minutes=15 >}}

## System prompt best practices

-   Be explicit about role, audience, and format
-   Show, don't just tell ("few-shot" examples)
-   Tell the model how to handle uncertainty
-   Read more on how [When plotting, LLMs see what they expect to see](https://posit.co/blog/introducing-bluffbench)

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `11-quiz-game-app` and write the system prompt for our quiz game show. The app reads your prompt from `11-quiz-game-prompt.md`; run it to play.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=15 >}}

# Manual RAG {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Context in, better answers out

-   "Retrieval-Augmented Generation" without any fancy infrastructure
-   Stuff the relevant docs into the prompt yourself
-   Most "RAG" is this idea plus automation

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `12-coding-assistant` and try a coding assistant on a niche package (R: `weathR`, Python: `NWS`):

-   First, ask with no context and note what goes wrong.
-   Then paste the README into the prompt and compare.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

# RAG {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## How RAG actually works

-   Chunk your documents
-   Compute embeddings for each chunk
-   At query time, find the most similar chunks and add them to the prompt
-   The "retrieval" is just nearest-neighbor search

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `13-rag` and build a dynamic RAG system:

-   Source documents: R for Data Science (R) or Polars Cookbook (Python).
-   Tools: `ragnar` (R), `llama-index` or chatlas helpers (Python).
-   Wire retrieval up as a tool the model can call.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=20 >}}

# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/)
:::
